rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend First Amended Petition For Order Directing Conveyance Or Transfer Of Property To Probate Estate
rulings.Rulings: Tentative ruling by Judge Ross C. Moody. Petitioner Mary Bong's Motion to Amend First Amended Petition is GRANTED. Petitioner Mary Bong filed her Petition for Order Directing Conveyance or Transfer of Property to Probate Estate on January 8, 2021. A First Amended Petition was filed on April 8, 2021. Petitioner seeks leave to amend on three grounds. First, Petitioner seeks to add the unpaid balance of a loan to her pending claims brought under Probate Code sec 850. Petitioner contends that the outstanding loan principal is an asset of Decedent's intestate estate. As part of this new claim, Petitioner seeks to add, as new parties, Annie Tang, in her capacity as Successor Trustee of Fortune 8 Trust, Betty Bong and James Bong Enterprise Inc. Second, Petitioner seeks to add material to her existing claim regarding Decedent's account designation forms at Citibank, which Petitioner alleges are not genuine. Finally, Petitioner seeks to add a new cause of action under Civil Code sec 3412 and 3439 to set aside or annul certain loans made by the Fortune 8 Trust. Respondent opposes the motion challenging the amendments, generally, as untimely. The Court finds no showing of unwarranted delay in adding claims or bringing a new cause of action following the discovery of new facts; nor does the Court find any prejudice to the opposing party in allowing them to be pled. Trial has not been set in this matter and discovery is ongoing. Judicial policy favors resolution of all disputed matters in the same lawsuit. (Kittredge Sports Co. v. Superior Court (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1047.) Moreover, "[t]he rule is well established that great liberality will be used  where an amendment provides 'merely the addition of matters essential to make the original cause of action complete'. (Citation.)" (In re Hunter's Estate (1961) 194 Cal.App.2d 859, 865; Higgins v. Del Faro (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 558, 564-65.) Petitioner is ordered to file her Second Amended Petition within five court days of entry of this order. The Trial Setting conference set for September 25, 2023 is vacated. A hearing on the Second Amended Petition and a Case Management Conference is hereby set for October 16, 2023 at 9 a.m. in Department 204 of this court. The parties are ordered to file an updated Joint Case Management Statement on or before October 9, 2023. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
